Ruling:
DEFENDANTS KARMI OBERWAGER's and LIN VELASQUEZ's Motion For Attorney's Fees
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANTS KARMI OBERWAGER's and LIN VELASQUEZ'S Motion For Attorney's Fees. The motion is denied. Plaintiff had a prima facie basis for the retaliation claim which was viable until the decision Jones v. The Lodge at Torrey Pines (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1158. Plaintiff's other claims were linked to the retaliation claim. Plaintiff was unable to present substantial responsive evidence demonstrating a retaliatory animus, but that is not a basis for awarding fees. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Defendant California Pacific Medical Center, Karmi Oberwager, Lin Velasquez and Elizabeth Avants' motion for a protective order to prevent the taking of the deposition of Lisa Buhler is denied without prejudice to defendant asserting objections during the deposition to particular questions based on any privilege that may apply. The Court cannot determine from the motion and declaration that all questions directed to the witness would necessarily implicate either the attorney client or attorney work product privilege. (See: Wellpoint Health Networks Inc. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal. App. 4th 110). The Court finds that identifying the witness in responses to form interrogatories as having knowledge or participating in certain decisions did not constitute a waiver. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
